meta.title: G42 Engine Replacement Cost UK — Compare Quotes
meta.description: Every BMW 2 Series G42 engine — B48, B47, B58. Real UK costs, failure data, and honest advice on the latest 2 Series Coupé. Get free quotes from 20+ vetted BMW specialists today.

mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW 2 Series G42 engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned G42 engine typically costs £2,500–£7,500 supply only, depending on engine — the B48 four-cylinder sits at £2,500–£5,000, while the B58 straight-six can reach £4,500–£7,500. Fitting adds £650–£1,040. Use our calculator for a personalised figure.

mainEntity.name: Which BMW 2 Series G42 engine is the most reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The B58 3.0-litre straight-six in the M240i xDrive has developed a reputation as one of BMW's most dependable modern engines — robust, smooth, and well-proven across the G20 3 Series and G22 4 Series. The B48 four-cylinder is also well-regarded for its reliability and efficiency.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the BMW 2 Series G42?

acceptedAnswer.text: Water pump and cooling system failures on all B-series engines (B48, B47, B58), PCV valve issues on the B58, EGR cooler and DPF issues on the B47 diesel, and — unrelated to the engine — the occasional infotainment system glitches and 48V mild-hybrid system gremlins.

mainEntity.name: Is the BMW 2 Series G42 reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The G42 is still relatively new, but early indications are positive. The B58 engine has a strong reputation for reliability, and the B48 is well-proven from its use across the BMW range. The B47 diesel is also generally reliable, though it has the usual diesel emissions system complexity. Overall, the G42 appears to be a dependable modern BMW.

mainEntity.name: Which BMW 2 Series G42 engine should I avoid?

acceptedAnswer.text: No engine is inherently problematic, but the B47 diesel (220d) is more complex than the petrol options — if you don't do high mileage, the petrol B48 or B58 is a simpler choice. The M240i's B58 is a performance engine with higher running costs — budget accordingly.

mainEntity.name: How do I know if my G42 engine needs replacing?

acceptedAnswer.text: Overheating with no obvious external leak (cooling system failure), persistent check engine lights, rough idle, or increased oil consumption (B58 PCV valve) are the key warning signs. Use our diagnostic calculator for a personalised assessment.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a BMW 2 Series G42 engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: On a G42, replacement almost always makes sense. Against a £35,000 G42, a £5,000 B58 recon is 14% of the car's value — well within our 70% BMW threshold. Even against a lower-value example, the G42's relatively high retained value means replacement is financially viable.

mainEntity.name: How long do BMW 2 Series G42 engines last?

acceptedAnswer.text: The B58 can exceed 150,000–200,000 miles with proper maintenance. The B48 is similarly durable. The B47 diesel can also reach high mileage — but requires regular servicing and DPF maintenance. The G42's engines are built on well-proven architectures.

mainEntity.name: What is the difference between the G42 and the F22?

acceptedAnswer.text: The G42 is the second-generation 2 Series Coupé (2021–present), built on the CLAR platform with a larger footprint, more advanced technology, and a range of B-series engines. The F22 is the first-generation 2 Series Coupé (2014–2021), built on the older E9x-derived platform with N-series engines. The G42 is significantly more refined and technologically advanced.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my BMW 2 Series G42 engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation; block stamping is visible on the engine itself. Tell us your variant (220i, 230i, M240i, 220d) and we'll identify it — 20+ vetted specialists will confirm it for free.

hero.subHeadline: Every engine fitted to the G42 coupé — from the B48 four-cylinder petrol to the B58 straight-six in the M240i. Real UK replacement costs, honest failure data, and the B58's bulletproof reputation. Backed by verified specialist data.

engineDatabase.h2: BMW 2 Series G42 Engine Codes — The Complete Database
engineDatabase.subHeadline: Every engine fitted to the G42 coupé — from the 2.0-litre four-cylinder B48 to the 3.0-litre straight-six B58 in the M240i xDrive.

overview.intro: The G42 is the second generation of the BMW 2 Series Coupé, launched in July 2021 as the successor to the F22. Built on the CLAR platform shared with the G20 3 Series and G22 4 Series, it retains rear-wheel drive — unlike the front-wheel-drive 1 Series — and offers a 50:50 weight distribution. Production began in September 2021 at BMW's San Luis Potosí plant in Mexico — the first BMW designed by BMW of Mexico.
overview.keyFacts: Platform: BMW CLAR (shared with G20 3 Series, G22 4 Series) • Predecessor: BMW 2 Series F22 • Successor: Current model — no successor announced • Sibling body styles in this generation: Coupé only (no convertible)
overview.marketIntelligenceLine: As a relatively new model (2021–present), the G42 is still establishing its long-term reliability profile — but the B58 engine in the M240i is already developing a reputation as one of BMW's most dependable modern six-cylinders.

items.quote: The G42's engine range is modern, efficient, and broadly dependable. The B58 3.0-litre straight-six in the M240i xDrive is the standout — 374–382 bhp, 500 Nm of torque, and a reputation for bulletproof reliability that has made it a favourite across the BMW range. The B48 2.0-litre four-cylinder (220i, 230i) is the volume choice — responsive, efficient, and well-proven from its use across the G20 3 Series and G22 4 Series. The B47 2.0-litre diesel (220d) offers 190 bhp and strong economy, with mild-hybrid assistance for improved efficiency. All G42 engines are paired with an eight-speed Steptronic automatic transmission — no manual is available. If you're buying a G42, the B58 is the emotional choice; the B48 is the sensible one; the B47 is for high-mileage drivers.

rows.ownershipVerdict: Best Petrol 4-Cyl — more power, same reliability

rows.commonMajorFailure: PCV valve, water pump, coolant leaks
rows.ownershipVerdict: Best Overall — the B58 is a modern legend

economicsRule.title: The G42 rule
economicsRule.text: A £4,500–£7,500 B58 recon against a £35,000 G42 sits at 13–21% of the car's value — well within our 70% BMW threshold. Against a £50,000 example, the same recon drops to 9–15% — a routine maintenance decision. On a G42, replacement almost always makes sense.

keyTakeaways.question: Which G42 Costs Least To Own?
keyTakeaways.answer: B48 220i — simpler than the B58, lower running costs.

keyTakeaways.question: Which G42 Costs Most To Own?
keyTakeaways.answer: B58 M240i — more complex, higher servicing costs.

keyTakeaways.question: Which G42 Is Best Value?
keyTakeaways.answer: B48 230i — six-cylinder performance feel on a four-cylinder budget.

problems.title: B48/B58 Cooling System & Water Pump Failure
problems.rootCause: BMW's B-series engines use an electric water pump that has a finite service life. The water pump can fail without warning, causing overheating. The cooling system also has plastic components (thermostat housing, coolant pipes) that can become brittle with age and heat cycling.
problems.recommendation: Replace the water pump proactively at 60,000–80,000 miles on any G42 — a £400–£800 preventative job is far cheaper than a £2,500+ engine replacement. Monitor the temperature gauge and investigate any coolant loss immediately.

cta.label: Compare B48/B58 engine prices for your G42 →

problems.title: B58 PCV Valve & Oil Consumption
problems.rootCause: The B58's Positive Crankcase Ventilation (PCV) valve can fail, causing increased oil consumption, rough idle, and check engine lights. The PCV valve is integrated into the valve cover on some B58 variants, making replacement more expensive than on earlier BMW engines.
problems.recommendation: Monitor oil consumption on your M240i — if you're topping up more than 1 litre between services, have the PCV system inspected. A £150–£300 inspection is far cheaper than a £800+ valve cover replacement.

cta.label: Compare B58 engine prices for your M240i →

problems.title: B47 Diesel EGR Cooler & DPF Issues
problems.rootCause: The B47 diesel's EGR cooler can develop internal leaks, allowing coolant to enter the exhaust system and potentially cause engine damage. The DPF can also clog on vehicles used primarily for short urban journeys.
problems.recommendation: If your 220d is used primarily for short journeys, budget for regular DPF regeneration and consider an extended motorway run every few weeks. Have the EGR cooler inspected at every major service.

cta.label: Compare B47 engine prices for your 220d →
